title: Arrabidaea carichanensis
intro: Arrabidaea carichanensis, also known as the Andean passionflower, is a plant species in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, as well as its ability to treat insomnia and other sleep disorders. The bioactive constituents of Arrabidaea carichanensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts that are used in dietary supplements and traditional remedies.
